zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Anne Goldstein於1515年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其時，她的父親Ashton Goldstein35歲，母親Miss de Bourgogne31歲。她於1538年與John George在英國諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1556年在英國英格蘭艾可傑爾姆斯福德享年41歲。

nameMeanings.name: Goldstein
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Isadore, Emanuel, Myer, Ari, Aron, Avram, Dov, Mort.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name composed of German Gold ‘gold’ + Stein ‘stone, rock’.

German: from a medieval personal name, nickname, or occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Low German golste(i)n ‘gold stone, precious ston (probably referring to chrysolite or topaz, which was used as a testing stone by alchemists).

variants.name: Goldstone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Malki, Menachem, Moshe, Yaakov, Yitzchok.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Anne Goldstein was born in 1515, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Ashton Goldstein, was 35 and her mother, Miss de Bourgogne, was 31. She married John George in 1538, in Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1556, in Chelmsford, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

Elle a épousé John George en 1538, à Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1556, à Chelmsford, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Anne Goldstein(이)가 1515년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ashton Goldstein(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Miss de Bourgogne(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1538년 영국 노르폴크에서 John George(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1556년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.
